title: v7 fails to compile due to Tab+SFSymbol.swift on Xcode 16.4
author: philprime
body:
I tried to update to v7 in [this pull request](https://github.com/techprimate/Flinky/pull/46#issuecomment-3485487396) but it fails to compile:

```
$ xcodebuild -project Flinky.xcodeproj -scheme App -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,OS=18.6,name=iPhone 16 Pro" build | xcbeautify

...

[SFSafeSymbols] Compiling Tab+SFSymbol.swift
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:87:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:value:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, value: value,  content: content)
             ^
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:117:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:value:role:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, value: value, role: role, content: content)
             ^
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:145:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:value:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, value: value, content: content)
             ^
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:175:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:value:role:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, value: value, role: role, content: content)
             ^
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:236:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, content: content)
             ^
❌ /Users/philip/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/Flinky-fjbekzfdacmofcbvaeurhpghphar/SourcePackages/checkouts/SFSafeSymbols/Sources/SFSafeSymbols/Initializers/SwiftUI/Tab+SFSymbol.swift:250:14: initializer 'init(_:systemImage:role:content:)' requires that 'LocalizedStringResource' conform to 'StringProtocol'
        self.init(titleResource, systemImage: systemSymbol.rawValue, role: role, content: content)
             ^

...

** BUILD FAILED **
```

On a side-note I would recommend you to set up a GitHub Actions workflow in this repository so your library doesn't fail to compile after release.

title: Image size
author: ashevin
body: There does not appear to be a way to configure an image size on macOS, akin to the export options available in the SF Symbols app.

title: Add a script to migrate a project to SFSafeSymbols
author: ChaosCoder
body:
Hey,

I wanted to migrate a project to use SFSafeSymbols, but the tedious task of replacing all occurrences one by one put me off, so I did what engineers do and wrote a script that automates the process.

**migrate.sh**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

if [ -z "$1" ]; then
  echo "Usage ./migrate.sh <folder>"
  exit 1
fi

grep -rl 'systemName:' $1 --include="*.swift" | while read -r file; do
  echo "Replacing occurrences in $file"

  # Make temporary file
  temp_file=$(mktemp)

  # Replace all `systemName: "some.key"` with `systemName: .someKey` (and variances)
  perl -p -e '
    s/\(systemName: "(\w+)(\.(\w+))(\.(\w+))(\.(\w+))"(,?.*)\)!?/\(systemSymbol: .$1\u$3\u$5\u$7$8\)/g;
    s/\(systemName: "(\w+)(\.(\w+))(\.(\w+))"(,?.*)\)!?/\(systemSymbol: .$1\u$3\u$5$6\)/g;
    s/\(systemName: "(\w+)(\.(\w+))"(,?.*)\)!?/\(systemSymbol: .$1\u$3$4\)/g;
    s/\(systemName: "(\w+)"(,?.*)\)!?/\(systemSymbol: .$1$2\)/g;
  ' "$file" > "$temp_file"

  # Check if the file has been modified
  if ! cmp -s "$file" "$temp_file"; then
    # Write the import statement to the top of the file
    sed -i '' '1s;^;import SFSafeSymbols\n;' "$temp_file"
    mv "$temp_file" "$file"
  fi
done
```

Usage: `migrate.sh directoryOfYourProject`
E.g. `migrate.sh .` if you are inside your project directory.

This replaces all symbols with up to four groups with the safe syntax. I wonder if you want to add the script (and possibly extend it for all cases, I don't know how many groups there can be max) to make the migration easier? If not, at least people might find this and use it (on their own risk). 🙂

title: Tab extensions (iOS18)

body: `Tab` has received a bunch of new constructors taking a `systemImage` string in iOS18. Would be great to have the respective extensions in SFSafeSymbols.

body: Title says it all, iOS and other OSes added new SF Symbols and changed a few ones... It would be awesome to make the new SF Symbols available via SFSafeSymbol

title: Slow build time
author: ajkiosd
body: A clean build of the project using SFSafeSymbols SPM takes 28 sec in M2 Max (Xcode 15.2) The entire project itself takes 18 sec (**30,000** line swift code). Also takes more than 1:20 sec when compiled in Xcode Cloud: So the suggestion is to work on compilation time to make it better. Thanks

title: Cannot add package
author: Larry-Gensch
body: I am trying to install SFSafeSymbols version 5.2, and Xcode is having a wee bit of a problem. I select the package: - Dependency Rule: Up to Next Major Version (5.2.0 < 6.0.0) - Add to Project: (my project) Result: Package Resolution Failed: - SFSafeSymbols - Updating from https://github.com/SFSafeSymbols/SFSafeSymbols - the package manifest at '/Packages.swift' cannot be accessed (Package.swift doesn't exist in the file system) ## (Screen captures below - Selecting the package: - Error message: I understand that this could be an issue with my environment and not an issue with SFSafeSymbols, but any assistance with this would be greatly appreciated.

title: Support for visionOS 1.0
author: sebj
body:
It'd be very helpful if symbols, and the custom initializer extensions, could be marked as available on visionOS 1.0.

Thanks!

title: Support for SF Symbols 5
author: FelixLisczyk
body:
Are there any plans to support SF Symbols 5? This new version was officially released a few weeks ago.

https://developer.apple.com/sf-symbols/

Thank you very much for this great library! I use it in all of my apps. ❤️

title: Add a secured nullable constructor for SFSymbol.
author: ShikiSuen
body:
Currently, if I want to use circled numbers in a for-loop, I have to use this approach:
```
                                    let matchedSymbol = SFSymbol(rawValue: "\(index + 1).circle")
                                    if SFSymbol.allSymbols.contains(matchedSymbol) {
```

What if SFSymbol(maybeRawValue: String) which returns a nullable SFSymbol?
This can simplify the things above into:

``
if let matchedSymbol = SFSymbol(maybeRawValue: "\(index + 1).circle") {
``

FYI.

title: Support Right-to-Left localization
author: n-studio
body: For some symbols such as `arrow.up.backward.and.arrow.down.forward.circle`, SF Symbol suggests to use Right-to-Left localization to flip the symbol horizontally. But `.arrowUpBackwardAndArrowDownForwardCircle.localized(to: .rtl)` will return `nil`. Is Right-to-Left not supported?

title: Supporting custom SFSymbols
author: ramink
body:
This looks like a great and useful project. I'm going to try to use it in a personal project.

Looking ahead, though, I can see myself making my own custom additions to SF Symbols. I haven't tried this yet, but Apple [documents](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiimage/creating_custom_symbol_images_for_your_app/) how you would do this. Does SFSafeSymbols already give us a way to extend its support to our own custom additions?
